2604.20835.title: Parallel-SFT: Improving Zero-Shot Cross-Programming-Language Transfer for Code RL
2604.20835.paper_summary: Modern language models demonstrate impressive coding capabilities in common programming languages (PLs), such as C++ and Python, but their performance in lower-resource PLs is often limited by training data availability. In principle, however, most programming skills are universal across PLs, so the capability acquired in one PL should transfer to others. In this work, we propose the task of zero-shot cross-programming-language transfer for code RL. We find that, for Llama-3.1, RL training for code generation in a source PL fails to improve, and sometimes even degrades, the performance on other target PLs. To address this, we hypothesize that effective RL transfer requires a generalizable SFT initialization before RL. We thus propose **Parallel-SFT**, an SFT strategy that incorporates "parallel programs" -- functionally equivalent code implemented in multiple PLs -- into the data mixture. We demonstrate that this improves transferability: when we subsequently perform RL on our Parallel-SFT model, we observe better generalization to unseen PLs. Analysis of the model internal representations reveals that Parallel-SFT leads to a more functionality-centric latent space, where equivalent programs across PLs are more tightly clustered, which we hypothesize to contribute to the improved transferability.
2604.20835.paper_summary_zh: 現代語言模型在常見的程式語言（PLs）如 C++ 和 Python 中展示了令人印象深刻的編碼能力，但它們在低資源 PLs 中的表現往往受到訓練數據可用性的限制。理論上來說，大多數程式技能在 PLs 之間是通用的，因此在一種 PL 中獲得的能力應該能轉移到其他 PL 中。在這項工作中，我們提出了代碼強化學習的零樣本跨程式語言轉移任務。我們發現，對於 Llama-3.1，在源 PL 中進行的代碼生成強化學習訓練未能改善，甚至有時會降低在其他目標 PL 上的表現。為了解決這個問題，我們假設有效的強化學習轉移需要在強化學習之前進行可泛化的 SFT 初始化。因此，我們提出了 **Parallel-SFT**，這是一種 SFT 策略，將“平行程式”——在多個 PL 中實現的功能等價代碼——納入數據混合中。我們證明這提高了可轉移性：當我們隨後對我們的 Parallel-SFT 模型進行強化學習時，我們觀察到對未見 PL 的更好泛化。對模型內部表徵的分析顯示，Parallel-SFT 使得功能中心的潛在空間更加緊密，其中跨 PL 的等價程式聚集得更緊密，我們假設這有助於提高可轉移性。
2604.20835.author: Zhaofeng Wu et.al.
2604.20835.authors: Zhaofeng Wu, Shiqi Wang, Boya Peng, Anuj Goyal, Melanie Kambadur, Sebastian Ruder, Yoon Kim, Chloe Bi
